skill.name: geo-content-brief-skill
skill.description: 将 GEO 内容访谈、关键词笔记、竞品线索和渠道约束整理成可执行的中文内容简报。用于团队复用、选题规划、内容生产交接和质量评审；不用于直接代写完整长文或替代品牌策略判断。
skill.summary: Generates structured Chinese GEO content briefs from interview notes, keyword research, competitor signals, and channel constraints.
skill.author: yaojingang

security_audit.summary: The skill is a legitimate content brief generator for Chinese GEO content workflows. All 918 static findings are false positives: 'Ruby/shell backtick execution' triggers are markdown code formatting characters in documentation files, 'weak cryptographic algorithm' flags are base64/hex-encoded data strings in JSON config files, 'high entropy' findings are normal language text in markdown reports, and 'path traversal' findings are URL fragments in generated HTML reports. No executable code with malicious intent was found. The interface.yaml explicitly sets remote_inline_execution to 'forbid', indicating safe design.

low_findings.title: False positive: Backtick characters in markdown documentation
low_findings.description: Static scanner flagged backtick characters in README.md and SKILL.md as 'Ruby/shell backtick execution'. These are standard markdown code formatting characters used to reference file paths like `SKILL.md` and `references/brief-structure.md`. No shell execution occurs.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Context confirmed: all backticks wrap file paths and identifiers in markdown documentation. No executable code present.

low_findings.title: False positive: High entropy strings in JSON data files
low_findings.description: Static scanner flagged hash strings and encoded identifiers in JSON config files as 'weak cryptographic algorithm' or 'high entropy payloads'. These are standard SHA-style hashes and base64 IDs used in the skill's metadata schema, not encryption keys or malicious payloads.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: JSON data files contain standard configuration identifiers and content hashes, not cryptographic secrets or obfuscated payloads.

content.user_title: Generate Chinese GEO content briefs from research inputs
content.value_statement: Content teams struggle to convert scattered interview notes, keyword data, and competitor signals into actionable briefs. This skill structures that research into a one-page Chinese content brief ready for writers and editors.
actual_capabilities: Converts interview transcripts, keyword notes, and competitor signals into structured Chinese content briefs
Identifies target audience, content channels, keywords, and brand boundaries from raw input
Generates clear article structure with recommended angles, keyword placement, and evidence requirements
Produces gap lists identifying missing information before content production begins
Applies output quality guardrails to remove generic marketing language and unsupported claims
Supports trigger evaluation to distinguish brief generation from full article writing requests
limitations: Does not write full-length articles or complete marketing copy
Does not replace brand strategy decisions or competitive positioning judgment
Requires core inputs (audience, channel, keywords) before producing useful output
Will not fabricate search volumes, rankings, or third-party citations when data is missing

use_cases.title: Content marketing team brief creation
use_cases.description: Marketing teams can transform weekly research dumps into standardized briefs that writers can execute against without ambiguity.

use_cases.title: SEO agency client onboarding
use_cases.description: Agencies can quickly produce structured briefs for new clients by combining client interview notes with keyword research and channel requirements.

use_cases.title: In-house editorial planning
use_cases.description: Editorial teams planning a quarter of content can use this skill to standardize brief format across multiple contributors and topics.

prompt_templates.title: Basic brief from interview notes
prompt_templates.prompt: Generate a Chinese GEO content brief from these interview notes: [paste notes]. Target audience is [describe audience]. Primary channel is [channel]. Key keywords are [keywords].

prompt_templates.title: Brief with competitor context
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a content brief in Chinese. Input notes: [notes]. Competitor pages to differentiate from: [URLs or summaries]. Brand boundaries: [list]. Required evidence: [list].

prompt_templates.title: Brief with explicit boundaries
prompt_templates.prompt: Generate a GEO brief. Audience: [audience]. Channel: [channel]. Keywords: [keywords]. Banned claims: [list]. Required topics: [list]. Output format: one-page brief with gaps and next steps.

prompt_templates.title: Multi-source synthesis brief
prompt_templates.prompt: Synthesize a content brief from these sources: interview notes [notes], keyword research [data], competitor analysis [summary], channel constraints [list]. Identify gaps before drafting. Output in Chinese with clear structure for the writing team.

output_examples.input: Interview about B2B SaaS pricing pages. Audience: CMOs at mid-market companies. Channel: company blog. Keywords: SaaS pricing strategy, pricing page best practices.
output: 目标读者：中型企业 CMO，关注定价透明度
核心洞察：客户最关心定价页如何展示 ROI 和扩展性
推荐角度：3 个候选角度（透明定价、价值阶梯、对比表格）
文章结构：引言-问题-方案-证据-结论，1500-2000 字
关键词布置：标题、首段、小标题各出现一次核心关键词
证据需求：客户案例 2 个、行业数据 1 个
风险提示：避免未经核实的统计数据

output_examples.input: Notes from sales calls about competitor X. Keywords: alternative to X, X vs us. Channel: comparison landing page.
output: 目标读者：正在评估竞品 X 的潜在客户
核心洞察：客户在比较时最在意 X 的 3 个具体短板
推荐角度：诚实对比 + 迁移路径说明
结构：对比表格 → 迁移步骤 → 客户成功故事
关键词布置：H1 用 'X 替代方案'，H2 用具体场景词
证据需求：迁移案例、价格对比、客服响应时间数据
下一步：补充竞品 X 最新功能对比信息

best_practices: Always provide target audience, channel, and at least 2-3 keywords before requesting a brief
Include explicit brand boundaries and banned claims to avoid generic or risky output
Review the gap list in the output before passing the brief to writers
anti_patterns: Do not use this skill to request full article drafts or marketing slogans
Do not ask the skill to invent competitor rankings, search volumes, or customer quotes
Do not skip the input clarification step when audience or channel is unclear

faq.question: What languages does the output support?
faq.answer: The skill is designed for Chinese (Simplified) GEO content briefs. It is not intended for English or other language briefs.

faq.question: Can this skill write a complete article?
faq.answer: No. This skill produces structured briefs only. Full article writing is outside its scope and explicitly listed as a boundary.

faq.question: What inputs are required?
faq.answer: At minimum: target audience, content channel, and 2-3 keywords. Additional context like competitor notes and brand boundaries improves output quality.

faq.question: How does the skill handle missing data?
faq.answer: The skill produces a gap list identifying missing information and asks clarifying questions rather than inventing facts or statistics.

faq.question: Is this skill safe to use with confidential client data?
faq.answer: Yes. The skill processes inputs locally and does not transmit data to external endpoints. It explicitly forbids remote inline execution in its configuration.

faq.question: What formats does the output support?
faq.answer: Output is a one-page structured brief in Chinese Markdown format, including sections for audience, insights, angles, structure, keywords, evidence, and risks.
